source_docs.title: Massively parallel digital transcriptional profiling of single cells
Supplementary Information for massively parallel digital transcriptional profiling

oligos.name: Beads-oligo-dT (GEM-X Single Cell 3' Gel Bead v4 primer)
oligos.role: Gel-bead capture / RT primer; primes reverse transcription of polyadenylated mRNA and tags each transcript with a cell barcode and UMI

components.name: Poly(dT)VN
components.role: 30 nt anchored oligo-dT that primes polyadenylated mRNA

oligos.notes: From Oligonucleotide Sequences (Gel Bead Primers): 5'-CTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT-N16-N12-TTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTVN-3'. v4 gel beads also carry a Capture Sequence 1 primer (Feature Barcode), not used in the 3' Gene Expression protocol.

oligos.name: Template Switching Oligo (TSO) (Template Switch Oligo B)
oligos.role: Template switch oligo; adds a defined sequence to the 3' end of first-strand cDNA for second-strand priming

components.name: TSO
components.role: template switch; 3' rGrGrG (GGG) anneals to the untemplated C's of cDNA

oligos.notes: Reagent 'Template Switch Oligo B' (PN-2001027) is named in the protocol; the sequence shown is the canonical 10x Chromium 3' TSO (terminal rGrGrG folded to GGG). The exact sequence is not printed in the supplied documents.

oligos.name: cDNA Forward Primer (cDNA Primers, PN-2000089)
oligos.role: Bulk cDNA amplification forward primer; anneals to the partial TruSeq Read 1 handle
oligos.notes: cDNA Primers (PN-2000089) drive the post-GEM-RT cDNA amplification; the forward primer corresponds to the partial TruSeq Read 1 handle carried on the gel bead.

oligos.name: cDNA Reverse Primer (cDNA Primers, PN-2000089)
oligos.role: Bulk cDNA amplification reverse primer; anneals to the TSO handle at the cDNA 3' end

components.name: TSO handle
components.role: primes the template-switch handle added during RT

oligos.notes: Reverse primer of the cDNA Primers mix; matches the 5' portion of the TSO handle. Exact printed sequence not provided in the documents.

oligos.name: Illumina TruSeq Read 1 primer
oligos.role: Read 1 sequencing primer; reads the 16 bp 10x barcode + 12 bp UMI

components.name: TruSeq Read 1
components.role: standard Illumina paired-end Read 1 primer site

oligos.notes: Present in the Sample Index PCR product top strand immediately 3' of the i5 index.

oligos.name: Illumina TruSeq Read 2 primer
oligos.role: Read 2 sequencing primer; reads the cDNA insert

components.name: TruSeq Read 2
components.role: standard Illumina paired-end Read 2 primer site

oligos.notes: Its reverse complement (AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC) appears on the final-library top strand 3' of the cDNA insert.

oligos.name: TruSeq Read 2 adapter (ligated)
oligos.role: Double-stranded adapter ligated after fragmentation; supplies the Read 2 primer site

components.name: TruSeq Read 2 adapter (top / ligated to insert)
components.role: revcomp of TruSeq Read 2 primer; joins cDNA insert to the i7/P7 arm

oligos.notes: Added during GEX Adaptor Ligation (step 3.3). Top-strand form matches the AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC segment in the Sample Index PCR product.

oligos.name: Library PCR Primer 1 / Illumina P5 adapter
oligos.role: Sample Index PCR forward primer; adds the P5 flow-cell adapter and i5 sample index
oligos.notes: Supplied via Dual Index Plate TT Set A (i5) + Library Amp Mix; P5 begins the final-library top strand.

oligos.name: Library PCR Primer 2 / Illumina P7 adapter
oligos.role: Sample Index PCR reverse primer; adds the P7 flow-cell adapter and i7 sample index
oligos.notes: Supplied via Dual Index Plate TT Set A (i7) + Library Amp Mix; the final-library top strand ends in the reverse complement of P7 (ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG).

oligos.name: i7 Sample Index (Dual Index TT Set A)
oligos.role: i7 sample index; read as Index 1

components.name: i7 sample index
components.role: 10 nt i7 index, unique per Dual Index Plate well

oligos.notes: Each Dual Index Plate TT Set A well contains one unique i7 and one unique i5. i7 index read = 10 cycles.

oligos.name: i5 Sample Index (Dual Index TT Set A)
oligos.role: i5 sample index; read as Index 2

components.name: i5 sample index
components.role: 10 nt i5 index, unique per Dual Index Plate well

oligos.notes: i5 index read = 10 cycles; located between P5 and TruSeq Read 1 on the top strand.

final_library.source_label: Chromium Single Cell 3' Gene Expression Dual Index library (Oligonucleotide Sequences, Sample Index PCR Product)
annotation_lines: AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC = TruSeq Read 2 adapter (revcomp of Read 2 primer)

evidence.locator: Chromium Single Cell 3' Gene Expression Dual Index library (Oligonucleotide Sequences, Sample Index PCR Product)

library_generation.title: GEM generation, gel-bead capture & mRNA priming
library_generation.summary: Cells are partitioned with barcoded gel beads into GEMs; released poly(dT) primers hybridize to polyadenylated mRNA.
library_generation.note: On the Chromium X, cells + RT master mix, gel beads, and Partitioning Oil B are loaded onto a GEM-X 3' chip. The gel bead dissolves, releasing barcoded poly(dT) primers whose 30 nt oligo-dT/VN anneals to the mRNA poly(A) tail.
library_generation.product:
|--5'- CTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT[CELL_BARCODE:16][UMI:12](T)30VN-3'
                                                  3'- A A A A A A...XXXXXXXXX mRNA -5'

library_generation.title: Reverse transcription (GEM-RT)
library_generation.summary: Reverse transcriptase extends the poly(dT) primer into first-strand cDNA, adding untemplated C's at the 3' end.
library_generation.note: RT Enzyme E copies the mRNA into barcoded first-strand cDNA (48 C, 45 min). At the 5' end of the transcript the enzyme adds a few untemplated C nucleotides (CCC), setting up template switching.

library_generation.title: Template switching
library_generation.summary: The TSO anneals to the untemplated C's and is copied, adding a defined handle to the cDNA 3' end.
library_generation.note: Template Switch Oligo B (TSO, ...ACATGGG with terminal rGrGrG) base-pairs with the CCC overhang; RT switches template and extends, appending the TSO handle to first-strand cDNA.

library_generation.title: cDNA amplification (bulk PCR)
library_generation.summary: GEMs are broken, cDNA is Silane/SPRI-purified, and full-length barcoded cDNA is PCR-amplified with the cDNA Primers.
library_generation.note: cDNA Forward (partial TruSeq Read 1 handle) and cDNA Reverse (TSO handle) primers amplify the barcoded, full-length double-stranded cDNA (Amp Mix, 11-13 cycles) to generate mass for library construction.

library_generation.title: Fragmentation, End Repair & A-tailing
library_generation.summary: Amplified cDNA is enzymatically fragmented, end-repaired, and dA-tailed.
library_generation.note: Fragmentation Enzyme shears the cDNA to an optimal insert size (32 C, 5 min), then end repair and A-tailing (65 C) add a single 3' dA overhang for adaptor ligation.

library_generation.title: Adaptor ligation (TruSeq Read 2)
library_generation.summary: The double-stranded TruSeq Read 2 adapter is ligated to the dA-tailed fragment ends.
library_generation.note: DNA Ligase joins the T-overhang TruSeq Read 2 adapter to the A-tailed cDNA (20 C, 15 min), supplying the Read 2 primer site for the downstream index PCR.

library_generation.title: Sample Index PCR (dual index) → final library
library_generation.summary: PCR with Dual Index TT Set A primers adds P5+i5 and i7+P7, producing the finished Illumina paired-end library.
library_generation.note: Library Amp Mix + a unique Dual Index TT Set A well add the P5 adapter and i5 index to the Read 1 arm and the i7 index and P7 adapter to the Read 2 arm; double-sided SPRI size selection yields the final dual-indexed 3' Gene Expression library.

library_sequencing.note: Primer anneals to the bottom strand and reads the 16 bp 10x cell barcode followed by the 12 bp UMI (28 cycles).
library_sequencing.diagram:
ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT----------------------------->  (Read 1 primer, 28 cycles)
5'- ...ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCTNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNTTTTTTT...TTVN-[cDNA]... -3'
3'- ...TGTGAGAAAGGGATGTGCTGCGAGAAGGCTAGANNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNAAAAAAA...AABN-[cDNA]... -5'
                                        |<---16 bp cell barcode--->|<-12 bp UMI->|

library_sequencing.primer: i7 index read primer (5'-AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC-3', the TruSeq Read 2 adapter arm)
library_sequencing.note: Primer sits on the Read 2 adapter and reads the 10 bp i7 sample index (Index 1). Read as the reverse complement of the top-strand i7 on the P7 strand.

library_sequencing.primer: i5 index read primer (5'-AGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGTAGGGAAAGAGTGT-3', the Read 1 adapter arm)
library_sequencing.note: Primer anneals to the top strand at the Read 1 handle and reads the 10 bp i5 sample index (Index 2). On reverse-complement instruments (NextSeq/NovaSeq) the i5 is read from the opposite strand.
Primer anneals to the top strand at the Read 2 adapter and reads the cDNA insert (90 cycles), extending toward the 5' end of the insert.

title: 10x Chromium 3' GEX v4
description: A droplet-based single-cell 3' gene expression assay (10x Genomics Chromium GEM-X Single Cell 3' v4). Single cells are co-partitioned with barcoded gel beads into nanoliter GEMs; poly(dT) primers on the gel bead prime reverse transcription of polyadenylated mRNA, tagging each transcript with a shared 16 bp 10x cell barcode and a 12 bp UMI, and template switching adds a defined handle at the 3' end. Barcoded full-length cDNA is amplified in bulk, fragmented, and built into dual-indexed Illumina paired-end libraries. Read 1 reads the cell barcode + UMI, Read 2 reads the cDNA insert, and the i7/i5 index reads carry the sample indices, enabling digital 3' transcript counting of thousands of single cells.

reference.label: Massively parallel digital transcriptional profiling of single cells

original_publication.title: Massively parallel digital transcriptional profiling of single cells

authors.name: Jason H. Bielas
authors.affiliation: Translational Research Program, Public Health Sciences Division, and Human Biology Division, Fred Hutchinson Cancer Research Center, Seattle, Washington, USA

throughput.summary: Droplet system profiling thousands of single cells per channel; up to 8 samples in parallel per run at ~50% cell capture. Foundational study collected ~250k single cells across 29 samples. The GEM-X v4 protocol targets 500-20,000 cells per sample using a pool of ~3,600,000 10x barcodes.
throughput.cells: 500-20,000 cells/sample (v4); ~50% capture; ~250,000 cells across 29 samples in the original study
throughput.dna: Minimum 20,000 read pairs per cell recommended

publication.statistical_model: Poisson loading of cells into GEMs (multiplet rate modeled from Poisson statistics); barcode error correction via posterior probability; genotype/chimerism inference via a binomial two-genome mixture model with Gibbs sampling

other.label: Original chemistry (2017 paper)
other.value: 14 bp barcode (~750,000 sequences), 10 bp UMI, 30 bp anchored oligo-dT; sequenced 98 bp Read1, 14 bp I7, 8 bp I5, 10 bp Read2 on NextSeq 500

other.label: Sequencer compatibility (v4)
other.value: MiSeq, NextSeq 500/550/2000, HiSeq 2500 (Rapid Run), HiSeq 3000/4000, NovaSeq 6000

data_processing.summary: Sequencing reads are processed by the Cell Ranger pipeline: sample-index demultiplexing, cell-barcode + UMI extraction and error-correction, STAR alignment of the cDNA read, UMI deduplication by (barcode, UMI, gene), and gene-barcode matrix generation, followed by PCA / k-means / tSNE clustering. De novo SNVs (Freebayes) called on the Cell Ranger genome BAM support multiplet detection and SNV-based genotype/chimerism inference.
data_processing.statistical_model: Poisson cell loading (multiplet-rate model); binomial two-genome mixture with Gibbs sampling for SNV-based genotype/chimerism inference
